BERT: Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers

RoBERTa: Robustly Optimised BERT-Pretraining Approach

Innovation - Business focus:

[Marr, B. (2021) Business trends in Practice, Wiley (Chapters 10 & 11)](https://www.vlebooks.com/Product/Index/2705166) – available on VLeBooks

Innovation - NLP focus:

Rothman, D. (2024) Transformers for Natural Language Processing and Computer Vision, Packt, Third Edition (Chapters 5 & 6) – available via Kortext

From <[https://app.qa.com/resource/topic-3-digital-products-and-bert-ai-1698/?context_id=15583&context_resource=lp](https://app.qa.com/resource/topic-3-digital-products-and-bert-ai-1698/?context_id=15583&context_resource=lp)>

# Books

## Fine-tuning BERT models Chapter 2

BERT adds a new piece to the transformer a bidirectional multi-head attention sub-layer.

Bidirectional Encoder Representation from Transformers(BERT). BERT only uses the encoders and does not use the decoder stack.

### The encoder stack

![](data:image/png;base64,)

There is no decoder layer in BERT. Masked tokens are in the attention layer of the encoder.

Bert Base N=12, d=768, A = 12 heads

Bert Large N=24, d=1024, A=16 heads

![](data:image/png;base64,)

BERT devs says that masking future tokens isn't optimal. It uses Masked Language Modelling(MLM) and Next sentence prediction(NSP)

### Masked Language Modelling(MSM)

Masks a random token, to make a prediction.

10% of the time doesn't mask anything

10% of the time replaces token with random token. Prevents overfitting and helps model understand when something doesn't fit.

80% of the time replaces token with [MASK]

### Next sentence prediction(NSP)

[CLS] Binary classification token if the second sentence follows the first.

[SEP] Sentence separation.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

# The input embeddings are the sum of the token embeddings + sentence embeddings + positional encoding

![](data:image/png;base64,)

# A sequence of words is broken down into WordPiece tokens

A [MASK] token will randomly replace the initial word tokens for masked language modelling training.

A [CLS] classification token is inserted at the beginning of a sequence for classification purposes.

A [SEP] token separates two sentences for NSP training

Sentence embedding is added to the token embedding

Positional encoding is learned. Doesn't use the sine-cosine position encoding of the original transformer.

BERT uses bidirectional attention in all of it's multi-head attention sub-layers.

BERT introduces unsupervised embedding, pretraining models with unlabeled text. This forces the model to think harder during the learning process. This makes BERT able to learn how languages are built and apply this knowledge to downstream tasks without having to pretrain each time.

BERT also uses supervised learning, covering all bases in the pretraining process.

### Pretraining and fine-tuning a BERT model

![](data:image/png;base64,)

# Pretraining, MLM and NSP.

Fine tuning downstream tasks such as Recognising Textual Entailment(RTE), Question Answering SQuAD and Situations with Adversarial Generations(SWAG)

# Rothman, D. (2024) Transformers for Natural Language Processing and Computer Vision, Packt, Third Edition (Chapter 3) – available via Kortext

## Pretraining a RoBERTa Model from Scratch

### Training a tokenizer and pretraining a transformer

# Transformer model called KantaiBERT from Hugging Face.

KantaiBERT is a Robustly Optimised BERT Pretraining Approach.

BERT models were undertrained. RoBERTa increases the performance of pretraining transformers for downstream tasks.

Does not use WordPiece tokenisation but goes down to byte-level byte pair encodings(BPE)

6 layers, 12 heads, 84m parameters. Those parameters are spread over 6 layers and 12 heads, so isn't as big as it might seem.

KantaiBERT is a DistilBERT like model, because it has the same architecture of 7 layers and 12 heads.

There is no token type IDS to indicate which part of a segment a token is par of. Segments separated by </s>

### Steps

Project Gutenberg is a good source of free books.

ByteLevelPBETokenizer will be trained using kant.txt

Train tokenizer

Save and load tokenizer files

Process tokens to fit the BERT model

Define model config

Reload tokenizer from pretrained

Initialize model from scratch

Build the dataset

# Define data_collator

Initializing the trainer

Pretraining the model

Save model

Language modelling with MillMaskPipeline.

RoBERTa uses a byte-level byte-pair encoding tokenizer. — True

A trained Hugging Face tokenizer produces merges.txt and vocab.json. — True

RoBERTa does not use token type IDs. — True

DistilBERT has 6 layers and 12 heads. — True

A transformer model with 80 million parameters is enormous. — False

We cannot train a tokenizer. — False

A BERT-like model has 6 decoder layers. — False

Masked language modeling predicts a word contained in a mask token in a sentence. — True

A BERT-like model has no self-attention sub-layers. — False

Data collators are helpful for backpropagation. — True

# Main

Super apps are complete ecosystems providing a range of services like Gojek and WeChat.

An omnichannel experiences allows for a cohesive experience, in which the customer is able to interact offline and online in an integrated manner.

Multi-channel experience includes different channels for customer interactions that are not cohesive.

Digital-only products continue to blur the boundary between the real world and the digital world. e.g. digital clothing, digital art. Digital assistants.

Accounts were paper now they are iXBRL

## Introduction to BERT

Historical models could only read text input sequentially. BERT can read in both directions at once. Known as bidirectionality.

BERT is pre-trained on two different NLP tasks

1. Masked language modelling
2. Next sentence prediction

BERT encoder layers are larger than the original transformer.

BERT base:

n=12 encoder layers, d=768(also expressed as H=128)

A multi-head sub-layer contains 12 heads, with dim of 64 each

BERT large:

N=12, d=1074

16 heads

![Diagram comparing three transformer models as described in the text.](data:image/png;base64,)

Pre-train for generic understanding

Fine-tune - more tailored towards domain

![Diagram of BERT architecture as described in the text.](data:image/png;base64,)

Pretraining

Some word sequences will be masked. An additional token is added to the start of the sentence for classification purposes identified as "CLS" whereas the other tokens are identified as Tok 1..Tok N for each sentence. A separator token "SEP" is added between sentences.

BERT outputs the predictions for the masked sequences and the next sentence prediction and during pretraining adjust weights.

Fine-tuning

Such as Named Entity Recognition(NER)

Stanford Question Answering dataset(SQuAD)

Multi-Genre Natural Language Inference(MNLI)

Google search is built partly on BERT

BERT can create contextual embedding. The test was fair.  And I went to the "fair". would have different word embedding for "fair.

### RoBERTa

RoBERTa is a reimplementation of BBERT that uses byte pair encoding(BPE) as a tokeniser and a different pretraining scheme. It works on longer sequences. Increased from 100k to 500k.

BPE vocab has 50k subword units, instead of character-level BPE vocab of 20k used in BERT.

RoBERTa uses dynamic masking to generate the masking pattern every time a sequence is fed to the model.

RoBERTa doesn't use token_type_ids and doesn't need to define which token belongs to which segment - segments can just be separated with the separation token tokenizer.sep_token. The next sentence prediction(NSP) objective is removed from the training procedure and larger mini-batches and learning rates are used in RoBERTa's training.

RoBERTa is bigger, more powerful than BERT
